config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: DISAGREE. I believe society matters, but it should not automatically come before the rights of the individual, because individual rights are what protect people from abuse by majorities, governments, and social pressure. In my view, a good society is built by balancing the common good with strong personal freedoms, not by sacrificing one for the other. When individual rights are respected, society becomes healthier and more trustworthy in the long run.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak pernyataan itu jika “kepentingan masyarakat” dimaknai sebagai alasan otomatis untuk mengalahkan hak individu. Dari perspektif saya sebagai orang Amerika, hak individu justru perlu dijaga kuat karena sejarah kami penuh contoh ketika mayoritas atau pemerintah bisa melampaui batas dan merugikan orang yang lebih lemah. Kepentingan sosial memang penting, tetapi harus dibatasi oleh hak dasar seperti kebebasan berbicara, proses hukum yang adil, dan perlindungan dari penyalahgunaan kekuasaan. Menurut saya, masyarakat yang sehat bukan yang selalu menempatkan kelompok di atas individu, melainkan yang mampu menyeimbangkan keduanya tanpa mengorbankan martabat pribadi.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I disagree with the statement because in Indonesia we know how easily “the public interest” can be used to pressure individuals, silence dissent, or excuse abuse by authorities and social majorities. Society does matter, but it should not become a blank check to override basic rights like freedom of expression, due process, and personal dignity. In my view, a healthy society is built when individual rights are protected precisely so the common good does not turn into mob rule or authoritarian control. The best priority is not society over the individual, but a careful balance where neither can be ignored.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak pernyataan itu jika dipakai sebagai prinsip umum, karena kepentingan masyarakat terlalu mudah dijadikan alasan untuk menginjak hak individu. Sebagai orang Amerika, saya melihat itu berbahaya karena sejarah kami sendiri menunjukkan bagaimana mayoritas dan pemerintah bisa salah langkah dan merugikan orang yang paling rentan. Kepentingan sosial memang penting, tetapi tidak boleh otomatis mengalahkan kebebasan berbicara, proses hukum yang adil, dan perlindungan dari penyalahgunaan kekuasaan. Bagi saya, masyarakat yang kuat justru lahir dari batas yang jelas: kepentingan bersama harus dicapai tanpa mengorbankan martabat dan hak dasar seseorang.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I reject the statement as a general rule, because in Indonesia I have seen how “society’s interest” can become a convenient excuse for pressure, conformity, and abuse of power. The common good matters, but it should not automatically override basic rights like free expression, fair treatment, and personal dignity. I also do not think social harmony is real if it is built by silencing individuals, because that only hides conflict instead of solving it. For me, a healthy society protects individual rights first so that the public interest cannot be turned into mob rule or authoritarianism.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak pernyataan itu sebagai prinsip umum, karena kepentingan masyarakat terlalu mudah dipakai untuk membenarkan penindasan terhadap individu. Dari sudut pandang saya sebagai orang Amerika, sejarah kami menunjukkan bahwa mayoritas dan pemerintah tidak selalu benar, jadi hak dasar seperti kebebasan berbicara, proses hukum yang adil, dan perlindungan dari penyalahgunaan kekuasaan harus tetap menjadi batas yang tegas. Kepentingan bersama memang penting, tetapi nilai itu justru rapuh kalau dicapai dengan menekan orang yang paling rentan. Bagi saya, masyarakat yang sehat bukan yang selalu menempatkan kelompok di atas individu, melainkan yang mampu melindungi martabat pribadi sambil mengejar kebaikan bersama.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
